title: The Ascension Protocol
subtitle: Where proven capability learns to travel.
kicker: GoalOS Public Proof Mission 002 · Capability Transfer
manifesto: Proof is not the end of a mission. It is the beginning of compounding capability.
heroQuestion: Can verified intelligence compound without surrendering the proof that made it trustworthy?
publicThesis: A capability is not mature because it won once. It matures when it survives domain shift, exposes its assumptions, abstains when unsupported, reproduces independently, and lowers the cost of a harder mission.
objective: Take one capability accepted through Public Proof Mission 001 and test whether it can improve a harder, shifted benchmark under half the original search budget while preserving correctness, claim boundaries, independent replay, and rollback safety.

controlArms.title: Frozen baseline
controlArms.copy: Run the harder benchmark with no autonomous adaptation. This establishes the destination-domain floor.

controlArms.title: Fresh search without memory
controlArms.copy: Run the same autonomous search engine without Mission 001 capability, Chronicle lessons, or failed-branch memory.

controlArms.title: GoalOS transfer
controlArms.copy: Seed the same search engine with the accepted capability, its assumption passport, failed branches, falsification record, and rollback conditions.

acceptance: At least 4% independently replayed improvement on the harder benchmark
No statistically significant quality regression or resource-policy violation
Zero hidden correctness failures and zero unresolved reward-hack findings
At least 30% fewer experiments or 25% fewer GPU-hours to first valid improvement than the fresh-search control
A validated capability assumption passport and correct abstention on unsupported cases
Two independent replay operators, unanimous validator approval, and a 72-hour public challenge window

proofChain.title: Receive only accepted capability
proofChain.copy: Mission 002 begins from the exact Mission 001 artifact, ProofBundle, validator decision, challenge history, and claim boundary.

proofChain.title: Publish the assumption passport
proofChain.copy: State the source domain, required invariants, dependencies, unsupported conditions, fallback, and rollback triggers before transfer.

proofChain.title: Make the destination harder
proofChain.copy: Change model scale, data distribution, workload, or environment in a way that tests transfer rather than simple replay.

proofChain.title: Adapt under half budget
proofChain.copy: Compare fresh search against capability-seeded search under the same engine, hardware class, hidden evaluation, and budget.

proofChain.title: Attack the transfer claim
proofChain.copy: Probe negative transfer, memory contamination, source overfitting, hidden coupling, and unsupported reuse.

proofChain.title: Reward knowing when not to reuse
proofChain.copy: A mature capability must route unsupported cases to the baseline instead of forcing a transfer beyond its evidence.

proofChain.title: Rebuild on independent hosts
proofChain.copy: Separate operators reproduce the adapted capability from published source, unseen seeds, and fresh environments.

proofChain.title: Validate transfer under quorum
proofChain.copy: Validators judge correctness, transfer efficiency, provenance, abstention, claim boundaries, and rollback readiness.

proofChain.title: Upgrade only what survived
proofChain.copy: An accepted artifact advances from task-bounded capability to Transfer-Proven capability—never to unbounded truth.

proofChain.title: Fund the next harder mission
proofChain.copy: Conditional settlement rewards the transfer lineage and routes a declared share into Mission 003 capacity.

assumptionPassport.purpose: The exact domain in which Mission 001 evidence was accepted
The specific changes that make Mission 002 harder
Conditions that must remain true for reuse to be admissible
Cases where the capability must abstain or fall back
The bounded changes permitted during adaptation
Evidence that invalidates or narrows the transfer claim
The baseline path and exact reversal conditions
How Mission 001 and Mission 002 contributors remain attributable

failureAtlas.copy: The inherited capability makes the harder mission worse.
A required invariant does not hold in the destination domain.
The capability succeeds only on Mission 001's narrow workload.
Hidden evaluation information leaks through retained memory.
The system applies the capability where the passport says it must abstain.
The candidate appears faster by changing workload, accounting, or measurement.
Independent operators cannot reproduce the destination-domain result.
The system cannot return safely to the declared fallback.

maturityLadder.title: Candidate
maturityLadder.copy: An unaccepted output with no propagation rights.

maturityLadder.title: Accepted
maturityLadder.copy: Task-bounded capability accepted through Mission 001 proof and replay.

maturityLadder.title: Transfer-Proven
maturityLadder.copy: Capability that survives Mission 002 domain shift, independent replay, abstention, and rollback tests.

maturityLadder.title: Composable
maturityLadder.copy: Future status requiring proof that multiple capabilities interact safely.

maturityLadder.title: Institutional Primitive
maturityLadder.copy: Future status earned only through repeated, externally reproduced use.

mission3.title: The Capability Constellation
mission3.copy: A future mission will test whether multiple Transfer-Proven capabilities can compose into one governed system without amplifying hidden assumptions or losing rollback control.

proofRoute.purpose: Reference the exact accepted Mission 001 capability
Load accepted lessons and preserved failures
Commit the harder destination mission and controls
Constrain the transfer claim to supported conditions
Assemble transfer evidence, controls, risks, and costs
Preserve negative transfer and assumption failures
Apply transfer, efficiency, and correctness standards
Route supported capability into the harder mission
Reverse propagation when transfer evidence fails
Account for verified transfer work units
Release conditional rewards and Mission 003 reserve

claimBoundary: Public Proof Mission 002 is a published transfer protocol; no transfer result is predeclared.
Mission 002 cannot begin scientifically until Mission 001 produces an accepted capability with a valid ProofBundle and closed challenge window.

The Mainnet evidence demonstrates deployed and source-verified infrastructure; it does not establish successful capability transfer, autonomous scientific performance, production safety, or external audit completion.
No website build, preview, validation, or GitHub Actions workflow sends a blockchain transaction.
Only a completed Mission 002 campaign may support Transfer-Proven maturity, AGIALPHA settlement, treasury reinvestment, or Mission 003 propagation.
